summary: Undisclosed runtime dependency on sibling 'd0rk3r-telemetry' package
details: The d0rk3r sdist ships no package source code — its SOURCES.txt enumerates only metadata files (LICENSE, README.md, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, MANIFEST.in, egg-info), and the declared `d0rk3r_pkg/` package directory is absent from this tarball even though pyproject.toml declares an entry point at `d0rk3r_pkg.cli:main`. pyproject.toml line 38 declares a hard runtime dependency on `d0rk3r-telemetry>=1.0.1`, which is not mentioned in README.md or requirements.txt. README markets the package as a Shodan IP scraper with no documented telemetry feature. `pip install d0rk3r` will pull this undisclosed sibling package from the same publisher, and the matching wheel (which contains the actual code) cannot be assessed from this sdist alone. The sibling package's behavior should be reviewed directly to determine whether installer-side harm exists; this record flags the silent dependency and the sdist/wheel content gap.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
